title: Methods for treatment of interstitial cystitis
abstract: The present application relates to methods and uses of CB2 target agent local anaesthetics such as beta-caryophyllene for treatment of interstitial cystitis in a subject, optionally together with the administration or use of an additional agent for treating interstitial cystitis such as dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO) or methylsulfonylmethane (MSM).
claims: Claims CLAIMS: 1 . A use of a CB2 target agent local anaesthetic for treating interstitial cystitis in a subject. 2. A use of a CB2 target agent local anaesthetic for preparation of a medicament for treating interstitial cystitis in a subject. 3. The use of claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the CB2 target agent local anaesthetic is a CB2 orthosteric agonist agent local anaesthetic. 4. The use of claim 3, wherein the CB2 orthosteric agonist agent local anaesthetic is beta-caryophyllene. 5. The use of any one of claims 1 to 4, further comprising use of an additional agent for treating interstitial cystitis in the subject. 6. The use of claim 5, wherein the additional agent is dimethyl sulfoxide. 7. The use of claim 6, wherein the dimethyl sulfoxide is for use intravesically as an aqueous solution comprising from about 5% to about 50% dimethyl sulfoxide. 8. The use of claim 7, wherein the aqueous solution comprises about 5% dimethyl sulfoxide. 9. The use of claim 5, wherein the additional agent is methylsulfonylmethane. 10. The use of claim 9, wherein the methylsulfonylmethane is for use intravesically as an aqueous solution comprising about 5% methylsulfonylmethane. 1 1. The use of claim 9, wherein the methylsulfonylmethane is for use orally. 12. The use of any one of claims 1 to 1 1 , wherein the CB2 target agent local anaesthetic is for use orally. 13. The use of claim 12, wherein the oral use is in the form of a capsule comprising the CB2 target agent local anaesthetic. 14. The use of claim 13, wherein the CB2 target agent local anaesthetic is for use in combination with methylsulfonylmethane and the capsule further comprises the methylsulfonylmethane. 15. The use of any one of claims 1 to 1 1 , wherein the CB2 target agent local anaesthetic is for use topically. 16. The use of claim 15, wherein the topical use is in the form of a topical cream comprising the CB2 target agent local anaesthetic. 17. The use of any one of claims 1 to 1 1 , wherein the CB2 target agent local anaesthetic is for use intravesically. 18. The use of claim 17, wherein the CB2 target agent local anaesthetic is for use in combination with dimethyl sulfoxide and the intravesical use is in the form of an aqueous solution comprising the CB2 target agent local anaesthetic and the dimethyl sulfoxide. 19. The use of claim 17, wherein the CB2 target agent local anaesthetic is for use in combination with methylsulfonylmethane and the intravesical use is in the form of an aqueous solution comprising the CB2 target agent local anaesthetic and the methylsulfonylmethane. 20. The use of any one of claims 1 to 19, wherein the subject is human. 21. The use of any one of claims 1 to 20, wherein the subject is female.
